pattern: If we ..., then we will ...
b1: If we leave now, then we will arrive on time.
If we study together, then we will pass the test.
If we save money, then we will buy a new car.
If we practice every day, then we will speak better.
If we clean the house, then we will feel happy.
b2: If we reduce our expenses, then we will increase our profit this year.
If we launch the product in May, then we will lead the market.
If we improve our communication, then we will avoid misunderstandings.
If we invest in training, then we will have more skilled workers.
If we follow the instructions, then we will complete the project safely.
c1: If we ignore the environmental impact, then we will face severe legal consequences.
If we adopt this innovative strategy, then we will gain a competitive advantage.
If we prioritize long-term growth, then we will secure the firm's stability.
If we address the systemic issues now, then we will prevent future crises.
If we leverage our data effectively, then we will optimize our workflow.

pattern: What would happen if ...?
b1: What would happen if it rains tomorrow?
What would happen if I lose my phone?
What would happen if we are late?
What would happen if the shop is closed?
What would happen if he doesn't come?
b2: What would happen if we decided to change the project goal?
What would happen if the client rejected our proposal?
What would happen if we worked from home permanently?
What would happen if the budget was cut by half?
What would happen if a major competitor entered the market?
c1: What would happen if the global supply chain were to collapse entirely?
What would happen if we chose to ignore these ethical considerations?
What would happen if the regulatory framework changed overnight?
What would happen if there were a sudden shift in consumer paradigm?
What would happen if we integrated AI into our core decision-making?

pattern: Should the outcome be ..., we must ...
b1: Should the outcome be bad, we must try again.
Should the outcome be good, we must celebrate.
Should the outcome be a tie, we must play another game.
Should the outcome be positive, we must continue.
Should the outcome be a failure, we must learn from it.
b2: Should the outcome be negative, we must revise our current strategy.
Should the outcome be delayed, we must inform the stakeholders immediately.
Should the outcome be successful, we must expand our operations.
Should the outcome be uncertain, we must wait for more data.
Should the outcome be a loss, we must cut our expenses.
c1: Should the outcome be unfavorable, we must initiate our contingency plan.
Should the outcome be inconclusive, we must conduct a more rigorous analysis.
Should the outcome be controversial, we must prepare a formal statement.
Should the outcome be transformative, we must adapt our organizational culture.
Should the outcome be a breach of contract, we must seek legal counsel.

pattern: If I had known ..., I would have ...
b1: If I had known you were coming, I would have cooked.
If I had known about the rain, I would have taken an umbrella.
If I had known the time, I would have been early.
If I had known you were sick, I would have visited you.
If I had known it was free, I would have gone.
b2: If I had known the project was so urgent, I would have worked late.
If I had known about the traffic, I would have taken the subway.
If I had known his real intention, I would have refused the offer.
If I had known the software was buggy, I would have waited to buy it.
If I had known about the meeting, I would have prepared the report.
c1: If I had known about the fiscal discrepancies, I would have requested an audit.
If I had known the geopolitical risks, I would have advised against the merger.
If I had known the long-term implications, I would have chosen differently.
If I had known the research was flawed, I would have questioned the results.
If I had known the extent of the crisis, I would have acted more decisively.

pattern: Assuming that ..., it is likely that ...
b1: Assuming that it is sunny, it is likely that we will go out.
Assuming that he is busy, it is likely that he will be late.
Assuming that the car is fixed, it is likely that we can travel.
Assuming that you study, it is likely that you will pass.
Assuming that the shop is open, it is likely that I will buy it.
b2: Assuming that the market stays stable, it is likely that our sales will grow.
Assuming that everyone agrees, it is likely that the plan will succeed.
Assuming that we get the funding, it is likely that we can start in June.
Assuming that the technology works, it is likely that we will save time.
Assuming that she accepts the role, it is likely that the team will improve.
c1: Assuming that the current trend persists, it is likely that inflation will rise.
Assuming that the data is accurate, it is likely that we have found a solution.
Assuming that the legislation passes, it is likely that the industry will change.
Assuming that the merger proceeds, it is likely that we will dominate the sector.
Assuming that the theory holds, it is likely that the results will be replicated.

pattern: I predict that ... will happen.
b1: I predict that it will snow tomorrow.
I predict that our team will win the game.
I predict that he will be very happy.
I predict that the price will go up.
I predict that everyone will like the food.
b2: I predict that electric cars will become more popular next year.
I predict that the company will expand to other countries soon.
I predict that the interest rates will decrease in the next quarter.
I predict that remote work will change the way we live in the city.
I predict that this new technology will revolutionize the industry.
c1: I predict that a paradigm shift in consumer behavior will occur shortly.
I predict that the geopolitical tensions will lead to further economic instability.
I predict that the integration of AI will redefine professional roles globally.
I predict that the environmental crisis will necessitate radical policy changes.
I predict that the fiscal deficit will have long-term structural implications.

pattern: Unless ... happens, we won't be able to ...
kor: ...이 일어나지 않는 한, 우리는 ...할 수 없을 것입니다
b1: Unless it stops raining, we won't be able to play.
Unless you help me, I won't be able to finish.
Unless he comes soon, we won't be able to start.
Unless I find my keys, I won't be able to drive.
Unless we save money, we won't be able to travel.
b2: Unless we get more staff, we won't be able to meet the deadline.
Unless the weather improves, we won't be able to launch the boat.
Unless the manager approves it, we won't be able to spend the budget.
Unless we fix the bug, we won't be able to release the app.
Unless the client pays, we won't be able to continue the work.
c1: Unless the government intervenes, we won't be able to stabilize the market.
Unless there is a systemic reform, we won't be able to achieve equality.
Unless we secure further funding, we won't be able to complete the research.
Unless all stakeholders agree, we won't be able to implement the policy.
Unless the legal dispute is settled, we won't be able to finalize the merger.

pattern: In the event of ..., we should ...
b1: In the event of rain, we should stay home.
In the event of fire, we should use the stairs.
In the event of a mistake, we should say sorry.
In the event of a tie, we should play again.
In the event of an emergency, we should call 911.
b2: In the event of a power outage, we should use the backup generator.
In the event of a delay, we should notify our customers immediately.
In the event of a cancellation, we should offer a full refund.
In the event of a conflict, we should try to find a compromise.
In the event of a security breach, we should change all our passwords.
c1: In the event of a hostile takeover, we should consult our legal advisors.
In the event of a market crash, we should reconsider our investment portfolio.
In the event of a public scandal, we should issue a formal clarification.
In the event of a natural disaster, we should activate the crisis protocol.
In the event of a policy shift, we should adapt our strategic objectives.

pattern: There's a high probability that ...
b1: There's a high probability that it will rain.
There's a high probability that he will win.
There's a high probability that the shop is open.
There's a high probability that you will pass the test.
There's a high probability that we will be late.
b2: There's a high probability that the project will be completed on time.
There's a high probability that the stock market will recover next month.
There's a high probability that we will need to hire more people soon.
There's a high probability that the client will accept our new terms.
There's a high probability that technology will continue to advance rapidly.
c1: There's a high probability that the recent trends will lead to inflation.
There's a high probability that the proposed merger will face legal hurdles.
There's a high probability that systemic issues will hinder our progress.
There's a high probability that the new legislation will affect our profits.
There's a high probability that a paradigm shift is currently underway.

pattern: We might see ... if ...
b1: We might see a rainbow if the sun comes out.
We might see a bird if we go to the park.
We might see him if we wait here.
We might see a movie if we have time.
We might see some snow if it gets colder.
b2: We might see a rise in sales if we lower the price.
We might see more customers if we improve our website.
We might see a change in policy if the workers protest.
We might see better results if we change our strategy.
We might see a decrease in costs if we automate the process.
c1: We might see social unrest if the economic disparity continues to grow.
We might see a significant innovation if we foster a creative culture.
We might see a shift in power if the new legislation is implemented.
We might see a decline in biodiversity if we don't protect the forest.
We might see a global recession if the trade war escalates further.

pattern: This suggests that the future holds ...
b1: This suggests that the future holds more rain.
This suggests that the future holds happy days.
This suggests that the future holds new technology.
This suggests that the future holds more work.
This suggests that the future holds better cars.
b2: This suggests that the future holds many opportunities for our company.
This suggests that the future holds significant challenges for small businesses.
This suggests that the future holds a more digital way of living.
This suggests that the future holds a greater focus on health and wellness.
This suggests that the future holds a change in how we communicate.
c1: This suggests that the future holds a radical transformation of the labor market.
This suggests that the future holds a shift towards more sustainable energy sources.
This suggests that the future holds a complex geopolitical landscape.
This suggests that the future holds an increase in global interconnectedness.
This suggests that the future holds profound ethical dilemmas regarding AI.

pattern: The outcome depends entirely on whether or not ...
kor: 그 결과는 전적으로 ...인지 아닌지에 달려 있습니다
b1: The outcome depends entirely on whether or not it rains.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not he comes.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not you help.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not we win.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not it is free.
b2: The outcome depends entirely on whether or not we get the funding.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the client agrees to the terms.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not we meet the deadline.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the technology is reliable.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the team works together.
c1: The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the legislation is enacted.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not we address the systemic flaws.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the market stabilizes soon.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the merger is approved by the board.
The outcome depends entirely on whether or not the ethical guidelines are followed.

pattern: If the trend continues, then ...
b1: If the trend continues, then prices will go up.
If the trend continues, then we will have more work.
If the trend continues, then it will be very hot.
If the trend continues, then people will stay home.
If the trend continues, then the park will be crowded.
b2: If the trend continues, then our market share will increase significantly.
If the trend continues, then the company will need to hire more staff.
If the trend continues, then more people will shop online than in stores.
If the trend continues, then we will reach our annual goal by October.
If the trend continues, then the demand for electric cars will double.
c1: If the trend continues, then the socio-economic divide will become insurmountable.
If the trend continues, then the long-term fiscal stability of the firm is at risk.
If the trend continues, then a paradigm shift in urban planning will be necessary.
If the trend continues, then the environmental damage will be irreversible.
If the trend continues, then we will witness a total transformation of the industry.

pattern: Had they considered ..., the result would have been ...
b1: Had they considered the price, the result would have been better.
Had they considered the weather, the result would have been different.
Had they considered the time, the result would have been good.
Had they considered the rules, the result would have been fair.
Had they considered the children, the result would have been nice.
b2: Had they considered the risks, the result would have been more positive.
Had they considered the feedback, the result would have been much better.
Had they considered the competition, the result would have been more successful.
Had they considered the budget, the result would have been more realistic.
Had they considered the customer's needs, the result would have been profitable.
c1: Had they considered the legal ramifications, the result would have been favorable.
Had they considered the ethical implications, the result would have been different.
Had they considered the geopolitical climate, the result would have been stable.
Had they considered the long-term impact, the result would have been sustainable.
Had they considered the systemic vulnerabilities, the result would have been safer.

pattern: We need a backup plan in case ...
b1: We need a backup plan in case it rains.
We need a backup plan in case the bus is late.
We need a backup plan in case the shop is closed.
We need a backup plan in case he doesn't come.
We need a backup plan in case we get lost.
b2: We need a backup plan in case the main server fails during the launch.
We need a backup plan in case our primary supplier cannot deliver on time.
We need a backup plan in case the budget is not approved by the manager.
We need a backup plan in case the event needs to be moved indoors.
We need a backup plan in case the technology doesn't work as expected.
c1: We need a backup plan in case the merger faces unexpected regulatory hurdles.
We need a backup plan in case the geopolitical situation deteriorates further.
We need a backup plan in case the project's long-term funding is cut.
We need a backup plan in case our strategic partnership is dissolved.
We need a backup plan in case the new legislation negatively affects us.

pattern: It is conceivable that ...
kor: ...라는 것은 상상할 수 있는 일입니다(가능성이 있습니다)
b1: It is conceivable that we will finish early.
It is conceivable that he will win the race.
It is conceivable that the price will go down.
It is conceivable that she will say yes.
It is conceivable that it will be cold today.
b2: It is conceivable that the company will relocate its headquarters next year.
It is conceivable that we will need to update our entire system soon.
It is conceivable that the market will become more competitive in the future.
It is conceivable that the project will require more resources than planned.
It is conceivable that a new law will be passed regarding remote work.
c1: It is conceivable that the current economic trends will lead to a recession.
It is conceivable that a paradigm shift will occur in the energy sector.
It is conceivable that the merger will create a monopoly in the market.
It is conceivable that systemic failures will occur if no reform is made.
It is conceivable that the long-term effects of AI will be unpredictable.

pattern: Without ..., it is impossible to ...
b1: Without money, it is impossible to buy this.
Without a map, it is impossible to find the way.
Without water, it is impossible to live.
Without help, it is impossible to finish.
Without time, it is impossible to study.
b2: Without the manager's approval, it is impossible to start the project.
Without a clear strategy, it is impossible to succeed in this market.
Without effective communication, it is impossible to manage a large team.
Without sufficient funding, it is impossible to expand our operations.
Without the right tools, it is impossible to fix the broken machine.
c1: Without a robust regulatory framework, it is impossible to ensure safety.
Without addressing the root cause, it is impossible to solve the problem.
Without the stakeholders' cooperation, it is impossible to implement reform.
Without empirical evidence, it is impossible to validate the hypothesis.
Without long-term strategic planning, it is impossible to remain competitive.

pattern: The evidence points towards ... happening.
b1: The evidence points towards rain happening.
The evidence points towards him winning.
The evidence points towards a mistake happening.
The evidence points towards the shop closing.
The evidence points towards a fight happening.
b2: The evidence points towards a slow recovery of the economy happening.
The evidence points towards a shift in customer preferences happening.
The evidence points towards a significant increase in sales happening.
The evidence points towards a failure of the current system happening.
The evidence points towards a change in the management team happening.
c1: The evidence points towards a systemic breakdown of the network happening.
The evidence points towards a radical transformation of the industry happening.
The evidence points towards a long-term decline in market demand happening.
The evidence points towards a major geopolitical conflict happening.
The evidence points towards a paradigm shift in scientific thought happening.

pattern: On the condition that ..., we can proceed with ...
kor: ...라는 조건 하에, 우리는 ...를 진행할 수 있습니다
b1: On the condition that it is sunny, we can proceed with the picnic.
On the condition that you help, we can proceed with the work.
On the condition that it is cheap, we can proceed with the buy.
On the condition that we have time, we can proceed with the game.
On the condition that he says yes, we can proceed with the plan.
b2: On the condition that the budget is approved, we can proceed with the hiring.
On the condition that the safety checks are done, we can proceed with the launch.
On the condition that the client signs the contract, we can proceed with the project.
On the condition that we find a partner, we can proceed with the expansion.
On the condition that the technology is ready, we can proceed with the update.
c1: On the condition that the legal issues are resolved, we can proceed with the merger.
On the condition that the ethical guidelines are met, we can proceed with the study.
On the condition that the board agrees, we can proceed with the strategic shift.
On the condition that the market remains stable, we can proceed with the investment.
On the condition that the systemic flaws are fixed, we can proceed with the rollout.

pattern: The long-term implication is ...
b1: The long-term implication is more work.
The long-term implication is better health.
The long-term implication is a new house.
The long-term implication is being happy.
The long-term implication is a clean park.
b2: The long-term implication is a significant change in our business model.
The long-term implication is an increase in the cost of production.
The long-term implication is a more diverse and inclusive workplace.
The long-term implication is a stronger relationship with our customers.
The long-term implication is a decrease in our dependency on oil.
c1: The long-term implication is a fundamental shift in global power dynamics.
The long-term implication is the total erosion of digital privacy rights.
The long-term implication is the irreversible loss of several ecosystems.
The long-term implication is a paradigm shift in our understanding of labor.
The long-term implication is the necessity for a new moral framework in tech.
